summary: Real cover-letter-agent-generated approvals render an almost-empty review modal: the ApprovalRequest payload created by CoverLetterAgent.run() (apps/api/app/agents/cover_letter_agent.py:659-670) only sets kind/cover_letter_id/job_id/job_title/company, so the modal's 'Why approval is needed', 'AI reasoning', confidence badge, and — critically — the 'Generated cover letter' preview (the actual letter text) never render, because parseApprovalPayload() defaults all of them to null/[] when absent. A paying user reviewing a genuine AI-generated approval cannot see what they are approving in the one screen whose purpose is that review.
reproduction: Log in as admin/admin123 at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login
POST /agents/scout/run then GET /jobs to find a job id not already claimed by a pending approval
POST /agents/cover-letter/run {job_id} (async: poll GET /agents/jobs/{job_id} until status=completed) — note the returned approval_id
Navigate to /dashboard/approvals, filter Pending, click Review on the resulting card

Observe: no 'Why approval is needed' box, no 'AI reasoning' checklist, no confidence %, no 'Generated cover letter' preview text — only header, job title/company tile, trust checkbox, and action buttons
expected: Per design/screens/approval-modal.html, the modal shows why the approval fired, AI reasoning bullets, a confidence score, and a preview of the generated letter so the human can make an informed decision.
observed: For approval id c3df4494103f796021d8aae69 (real agent-generated, job 'Innovation Product Manager, Australia' @ harvey), GET /approvals/c3df4494103f796021d8aae69 payload = {kind, cover_letter_id, job_id, job_title, company} only. Live UI: whyVisible=false, reasoningVisible=false, confidenceVisible=false, previewVisible=false (confirmed twice, sessions 1 and 2).

summary: 'Edit & Approve' is permanently disabled for every real cover-letter-agent-generated approval, because ApprovalModal.tsx:326 disables it whenever details.preview === null, and (per finding 001) the real agent payload never sets a preview field. The wireframed edit-before-approve flow is unreachable for genuine data; it only works on hand-crafted payloads that happen to include a preview string.
reproduction: Open the review modal for a real agent-generated approval (see MV-approval-modal-001 steps)
Observe the 'Edit & Approve' button is rendered with disabled styling and is not clickable
Contrast with a payload that DOES include a 'preview' field (e.g. POST /approvals with payload.preview set): 'Edit & Approve' is enabled and the textarea opens correctly
expected: The wireframe's Edit & Approve action lets the reviewer tweak the generated letter before approving.
observed: editDisabled=true for the real approval's modal-edit-btn; editDisabled=false (and fully functional, verified with textarea fill/discard) for a synthetic payload with preview set.

summary: A long/unbroken string in an approval payload's job_title (or company) field breaks the page layout: no CSS word-break/truncate/overflow-wrap guard on the card title, so the whole /dashboard/approvals page (and the Dashboard's 'Needs Approval' widget, which reuses the same summarize() text) overflows horizontally, producing a page-wide horizontal scrollbar and a broken, unreadable layout for every card on the page, not just the offending one.
reproduction: POST /approvals with payload.job_title containing a long run of characters with no whitespace (e.g. 300x 'A')
Navigate to /dashboard/approvals (Pending filter) and to /dashboard
Observe the page body scrolls horizontally far past the viewport and every card layout is pushed/broken
expected: Card text should wrap or truncate; the page should never require horizontal scrolling regardless of payload content.
observed: document.body.scrollWidth = 3901px against a 1440px viewport (verified twice, sessions 1 and 2); visually confirmed in full-page screenshots on both /dashboard/approvals and /dashboard.

summary: parseConfidence() (apps/web/src/components/approvals/lib.ts) does not clamp out-of-range confidence values: a payload confidence of 1.5 (invalid — outside the documented [0,1] fraction range) is rendered as a nonsensical '2%' badge instead of being rejected/hidden.
reproduction: POST /approvals with payload.confidence = 1.5
Open the card/modal and read the confidence badge
expected: An out-of-range confidence value should be treated as absent/invalid (null), not silently rounded into a misleading percentage.
observed: Card and modal both display '2%' for confidence:1.5 (verified twice).

summary: Browser Back, while the review modal is open (opened via the deep-link ?review= param), does not close the modal — it navigates the user away from /dashboard/approvals entirely (observed landing on /dashboard) because syncReviewParam() uses history.replaceState, which never pushes a history entry for modal open/close. A user who reflexively hits Back to dismiss the dialog instead loses their place on the Approvals screen.
reproduction: Navigate to /dashboard/approvals, click Review on any card (URL becomes .../approvals?review=<id>)
Press the browser Back button
Observe the URL and page: it navigates to /dashboard (or whatever preceded Approvals in history), not back to a modal-closed /dashboard/approvals
expected: Back should close the review dialog and return to the Approvals list (or at minimum stay on /dashboard/approvals), consistent with standard modal/dialog navigation semantics.
observed: urlAfterOpen=.../approvals?review=<id> -> urlAfterBack=https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard; modalVisibleAfterBack=false. Reproduced identically in a fresh session.

summary: Deep-linking to a nonexistent approval id (/dashboard/approvals?review=<bad-id>) causes a real 404 from GET /approvals/{id} (confirmed in the server access log and network capture), and the frontend's catch handler does set an error message ('The linked approval request could not be found.') — but it is never shown, because the concurrent approvals-list load() effect's unconditional setError(null) on its own success wins the race and clears it. The failure is silently swallowed: the URL's review param is cleaned up and the page just shows the normal pending list with zero user-visible explanation.
reproduction: While logged in, navigate directly to /dashboard/approvals?review=nonexistent-id-mv
Wait >1s for both the list load and the deep-link fetch to settle
Observe: no modal opens, and no red/alert banner explaining the linked approval could not be found — the page silently reverts to the normal Pending list
expected: Per apps/web/src/app/dashboard/approvals/page.tsx:60-66, a failed deep-link fetch should surface 'The linked approval request could not be found.' in the page's error banner (role=alert).
observed: Network confirms GET /approvals/nonexistent-id-mv[-verify2] -> 404 {"detail":"Approval not found"} in both sessions, but getByRole('alert') / getByText(/could not be found/i) never becomes visible in either session (waited 700ms then 1200ms respectively).
evidence: /var/log/aether/api.log (line containing 'GET /approvals/nonexistent-id-mv-verify2 HTTP/1.1" 404 Not Found', read-only production log excerpt, not copied off-host)

summary: The modal's action-summary/preview section keeps cover-letter-specific copy ('Generated cover letter' label, 'CV'-style avatar fallback) even when the approval type is email_send (an outbound email, not a cover letter/application).
reproduction: POST /approvals with type=email_send and a payload of {to, subject, preview}
Open its review modal
expected: Section copy/iconography should reflect the actual approval type (e.g. 'Outbound email' / recipient+subject), matching the header subtitle which does correctly say 'Email Agent wants to send an email'.
observed: Header subtitle correctly reads 'Email Agent wants to send an email', but the tile still shows generic 'CV' initials + summary text 'Outbound email' (from summarizeType fallback) and the letter-preview box is still headed 'GENERATED COVER LETTER'.

summary: POST /approvals/{id}/execute (one of the 5 endpoints in this screen's matrix) is fully implemented and behaves correctly server-side (200 executed when approved; 403 when not approved; 409 when expired — all verified live), but has zero UI wiring: no button, link, or code path anywhere in the approvals list/modal ever calls it. The wireframe likewise has no separate 'execute' affordance.
reproduction: grep apps/web/src for '/execute' or an execute call in the approvals components — none found
Directly call POST /approvals/{approved-id}/execute, /{rejected-id}/execute, /{pending-id}/execute via the API and confirm the documented status codes
expected: N/A — flagged as a coverage note, not necessarily a defect (the endpoint's own docstring says downstream submission integrations land in a later phase).
observed: execute-on-approved -> 200 {"status":"executed",...}; execute-on-rejected -> 403; execute-on-pending -> 403. No frontend caller exists.

summary: A genuine production cover-letter generation (job 'Innovation Product Manager, Australia' @ harvey, approval c3df4494103f796021d8aae69) produced a grammatically broken opening sentence — 'My background as an I am a direct match for the Innovation Product Manager, Australia role at harvey.' This is caused by enforce_first_person() in cover_letter_agent.py over-matching: the seeded admin account has name=='Administrator' AND targetRole=='Administrator', so the deterministic hook clause 'as an Administrator' gets rewritten by the third-person-pronoun regex (which targets any literal occurrence of the signer's name) into 'as an I am', mangling a sentence where 'Administrator' was being used as a job-title noun, not a third-person reference to the candidate. Because of MV-approval-modal-001 (no letter preview shown in the real approval's review modal), this broken sentence was invisible to the human reviewer at approval time — it shipped into an approved, submitted Application untouched.
reproduction: POST /agents/cover-letter/run for a job where the resulting letter's deterministic hook includes the candidate's current position/title
Poll GET /agents/jobs/{id} until completed; read result.cover_letter
With the seeded admin/admin123 account (name='Administrator', targetRole='Administrator'), observe the hook sentence is corrupted
expected: The composed business letter should always be grammatically well-formed; enforce_first_person should only rewrite genuine third-person references to the candidate, not a coincidental noun match against the signer's own name string.
observed: cover_letter text begins: 'My background as an I am a direct match for the Innovation Product Manager, Australia role at harvey.' (should read '...as an Administrator is a direct match...'). Confirmed via the raw agent-run result and again via GET /applications/{cover_letter_id} after approval.
